name: Mykonos
summary: Mykonos is generally safe for tourists but has higher petty crime rates than most other Greek islands. While violent crime is rare, travelers should be aware of pickpocketing, tourist-targeted scams, and overcharging at certain establishments.
quickVerdict: Mykonos offers a relatively safe travel experience with world-class nightlife and beautiful beaches. The main safety concerns involve petty theft in crowded areas, overpriced restaurants that target tourists, and the typical risks associated with party destinations. Solo travelers, including women, generally feel secure, though standard precautions should be taken especially at night. The island has good healthcare facilities and reliable emergency services.

neighborhoods.name: Mykonos Town (Chora)
neighborhoods.description: The main town center is generally safe with good lighting and regular police presence. Watch for pickpockets in crowded alleys and tourist areas, especially around Little Venice.

neighborhoods.name: Paradise Beach
neighborhoods.description: Popular party beach with good daytime safety but increased risks at night due to heavy drinking and crowds. Keep valuables secure and be cautious of drink tampering.

neighborhoods.name: Super Paradise Beach
neighborhoods.description: Lively beach club area that's generally safe during the day. Nighttime parties can get wild - stay with your group and watch your drinks carefully.

neighborhoods.name: Psarou Beach
neighborhoods.description: Upscale beach area with luxury hotels and restaurants. Well-maintained with good security, though expensive. One of the safest areas on the island.

neighborhoods.name: Ornos
neighborhoods.description: Family-friendly area with good restaurants and beaches. Generally quiet and safe with less nightlife activity than other parts of the island.

neighborhoods.name: Ano Mera
neighborhoods.description: Traditional village in the island's interior. Very safe and authentic, offering a break from the tourist crowds with local tavernas and historic sites.

scams.name: Overpriced Restaurant Bills
scams.description: Notorious establishments like DK Oyster charge exorbitant prices without showing menus upfront, leading to bills of 500-800 euros for simple meals.
scams.howToAvoid: Always ask to see a menu with prices before ordering. If refused, leave immediately. Research restaurants beforehand and avoid places with aggressive touts.

scams.name: ATM Card Skimming
scams.description: Fraudsters approach tourists at ATMs offering help with transactions while secretly scanning cards and observing PIN numbers.
scams.howToAvoid: Never accept help at ATMs. Cover your PIN when entering it. Use ATMs inside banks when possible and check your account regularly.

scams.name: Photography Scam
scams.description: Someone asks you to take their photo, then deliberately drops and breaks their camera, demanding payment for damages.
scams.howToAvoid: Be polite but firm in declining to take photos for strangers. If you do help, be extra careful with their device and ensure it's secure.

scams.name: Distraction Theft
scams.description: Someone spills something on you or creates a commotion while accomplices pickpocket your belongings.
scams.howToAvoid: Be suspicious of unexpected accidents or overly helpful strangers. Keep valuables in inside pockets and remain alert in crowded areas.

soloFemale.overview: Mykonos is generally safe for solo female travelers with a welcoming atmosphere and strong tourist infrastructure. Most women report feeling secure walking alone during the day and in well-lit areas at night. The party atmosphere requires extra caution with drinks and late-night activities.
tips: Stay in central Mykonos Town for easy access to restaurants and nightlife without long walks
Book reputable accommodations and research areas beforehand - avoid isolated locations
Be extra cautious with drinks at beach clubs and bars - never leave them unattended
Join group tours or activities to meet other travelers and enhance safety
Trust your instincts and don't feel obligated to be polite if someone makes you uncomfortable

nightSafety.overview: Mykonos has a vibrant nightlife scene that generally operates safely, though the party atmosphere brings typical risks. Well-lit tourist areas are patrolled regularly, but some beach club areas can become chaotic late at night. Transport back to accommodations can be challenging after midnight.
tips: Stay in groups when possible, especially when bar hopping or at beach clubs
Plan your transportation back to your hotel in advance - taxis are scarce after 2 AM
Keep emergency numbers saved in your phone and ensure your device is charged
Avoid walking alone on dark roads between venues - use the bus or pre-book transfers
Watch your alcohol intake and never accept drinks from strangers

transport.metro: No metro system exists on the island.
transport.rideshare: Uber operates with limited options (Uber Taxi, Uber Comfort, Uber Van) but availability is scarce during peak hours. Alternative apps like iMove work better for island transport.
transport.taxis: Only 31 official taxis serve the entire island, making them extremely difficult to find, especially at night. Expect to pay premium prices (50+ euros for short rides) and long waits during peak season.
transport.tips: The local bus system (KTEL) is reliable and affordable for reaching popular beaches. Pre-book airport transfers or consider renting a scooter/car for maximum flexibility, though parking is challenging in town.

customs: Greeks value hospitality (philoxenia) highly - accept offered food and drinks graciously as refusal can be seen as insulting
Dress modestly when visiting churches and monasteries - shoulders and knees should be covered
Tipping 10-15% at restaurants is customary and appreciated by staff
Greeks often arrive 30 minutes late to social gatherings - this is normal and not considered rude
Avoid pointing your palm outward when speaking as this gesture is highly offensive in Greek culture

health.overview: Mykonos has excellent healthcare facilities for a Greek island, with several private clinics and a public hospital. Medical tourism services are available, and standards are comparable to major European cities.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout the island, though many visitors prefer bottled water for taste preferences.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations are required. Standard travel vaccinations (hepatitis A/B, tetanus) are recommended.
health.altitude: Sea level location with Mediterranean climate. Summer temperatures can exceed 35°C (95°F) - stay hydrated and seek shade during midday hours.

emergency.usEmbassy: U.S. Embassy Athens: +30 210 721-2951. The embassy is located at 91 Vasilisis Sofias Avenue, 10160 Athens. For emergencies involving U.S. citizens, contact the 24/7 line.

faq.a: Yes, Mykonos is generally safe for tourists with low violent crime rates. However, petty theft and tourist-targeted scams are more common than on other Greek islands. Basic precautions like securing valuables and staying alert in crowds are sufficient for most visitors.

Mykonos has an active nightlife scene that operates relatively safely in well-lit tourist areas. The main risks involve excessive drinking, pickpocketing in crowded venues, and difficulty finding transportation after midnight. Stay in groups and plan your route back to your hotel.

faq.q: Is Mykonos safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, solo female travelers generally feel safe in Mykonos. The island has good tourist infrastructure and welcoming locals. Exercise normal precautions, especially around nightlife areas, and stay in central locations for easy access to help if needed.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Mykonos?
faq.a: Avoid walking alone on unlit roads between venues at night, and be cautious at certain overpriced restaurants like DK Oyster that are known for scamming tourists. Remote beach areas after dark should also be avoided when alone.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Mykonos?
faq.a: Yes, the local bus system (KTEL) is safe and reliable for reaching popular destinations during the day. However, limited taxi availability and expensive private transfers can be challenging at night. Consider pre-booking transport or staying centrally located.
